title: hedyotis lindleyana var. glabricalycina
intro: Hedyotis lindleyana var. glabricalycina, also known as the yellow flowered Hedyotis, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family, native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, attributed to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems. In traditional herbal preparations, Hedyotis lindleyana var. glabricalycina is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
